name: Santa María de Huerta — circular
region_intro: Hiking around Santa María De Huerta offers a blend of historical significance and diverse natural landscapes. The region is characterized by its ancient landmarks, including a 12th-century Cistercian monastery and historic castles, set amidst Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and junipers. Trails often follow the banks of the Jalón River and lead to unique natural features like the Laguna de Judes. This area provides a variety of paths suitable for exploring its varied terrain. Best hiking trails around Santa María De Huerta The most popular hiking route is Way of El Cid (GR 160), a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This section follows part of the historic Camino del Cid, offering a journey through ancient landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino del Cid: Arcos de Jalón - Montuenga Castle Loop, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.4 km) path. This route includes views of the medieval Montuenga Castle and the surrounding terrain. Local hikers also love the La Laguna de Judes from Chaorna — round trip, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes to the unique karst wetland, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Hiking around Santa María De Huerta is defined by historical castles, Mediterranean forests, and the Jalón River. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Santa María De Huerta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 19 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa María De Huerta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa María De Huerta?
A: There are 8 hiking routes available around Santa María De Huerta, offering a mix of easy and moderate options for explorers.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Santa María De Huerta?
A: The trails around Santa María De Huerta are categorized as either easy or moderate. There are 4 easy routes and 4 moderate routes, making the area accessible for various fitness levels.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while hiking in Santa María De Huerta?
A: The region is rich in history. You can encounter several historic castles, such as the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montuenga de Soria Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which once guarded the pass between the plateau and the Ebro basin. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Funes&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a large rock mass, is another notable ruin. Many routes also pass by the 12th-century Cistercian Monasterio de Santa María de Huerta itself.

Q: Are there any unique natural features to see on the trails?
A: Yes, hikers can discover unique natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5999892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Judes&lt;/a&gt;, a rare karst wetland in the province of Soria. During wet periods, the Almuguera de Chaorna stream can create beautiful waterfalls known as Chorroneras near the town. Trails also wind through characteristic Mediterranean forests of holm oaks, junipers, and kermes oaks.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santa María De Huerta?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1265282684/santa-maria-de-huerta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Huerta — circular&lt;/a&gt; route offers an easy 4.6 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1265272513/camino-del-cid-arcos-de-jalon-montuenga-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid: Arcos de Jalón - Montuenga Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.4 km path that includes views of the medieval Montuenga Castle.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa María De Huerta?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for exploration, especially through the holm oak and juniper forests.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails that pass through Santa María De Huerta?
A: Yes, Santa María de Huerta is a significant point on two major long-distance trails. Sections of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056890353/way-of-el-cid-gr-160" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid (GR 160)&lt;/a&gt;, following the path of El Cid Campeador, pass through the area. Additionally, stages of the GR-86 Sendero Ibérico Soriano traverse the region, offering routes through mountains dotted with characteristic Mediterranean trees.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Santa María De Huerta?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the blend of historical sites, such as the ancient castles and the Monasterio, with the diverse natural landscapes, including the unique Laguna de Judes and the serene Mediterranean forests.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, with 4 easy routes available, Santa María De Huerta offers options suitable for families. The shorter circular routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1265282684/santa-maria-de-huerta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Huerta — circular&lt;/a&gt;, are generally good choices for a less strenuous outing.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: While specific public transport details for individual trailheads are not always readily available, Santa María de Huerta is a town with some regional bus connections. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to nearby villages or starting points of longer trails like the GR-86 or Camino del Cid sections.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Santa María De Huerta?
A: Most trails around Santa María de Huerta begin near the town or accessible rural roads. Parking is generally available in or near the town center, especially close to the Monasterio, which serves as a central reference point. For routes starting further afield, roadside parking or small designated areas near trailheads are common.
tips: Leaving the valley of the river Jalón and the town of Santa María de Huerta behind and entering the hills that surround the town, it feels as if time has stopped. The 'moon' scenery is beautiful, especially at sunrise and sunset.

The Cistercian monastery "Monasterio de Santa María de Huerta" is mainly built in an early Gothic style, although other styles can also be admired in places: such as the Herrera style, a Spanish style of the late Renaissance, in which the second cloister on the west of the abbey is kept.
